seo.title: AI Agent Observability in Vercel AI SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent observability in Vercel AI SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Vercel AI SDK, AI Agent Observability challenges arise from streaming responses that mix partial hallucinations with valid content, unvalidated tool call results displayed directly to users, inconsistent structured outputs across LLM providers, and edge function timeouts during complex agent tasks. These issues manifest uniquely in the SDK's `generateText` and `streamText` APIs, where real-time rendering bypasses traditional validation, leading to unreliable agent interactions. Proper observability is critical for production agents built with React Server Components and the SDK's agent patterns.

howItManifests.symptom: Partial token streams contain fabricated facts mid-response before correction
howItManifests.impact: Users see convincing but incorrect info in real-time UI, eroding trust

howItManifests.scenario: Tool call results not validated before display
howItManifests.symptom: Raw tool outputs (errors, empty results) rendered directly via `streamObject`
howItManifests.impact: Broken UIs and security risks from untrusted external data

howItManifests.symptom: OpenAI `tool_calls` vs Anthropic `toolUse` parsed differently by `streamObject`
howItManifests.impact: Runtime parsing failures crash agent flows unpredictably

howItManifests.scenario: Edge function timeouts on complex agent tasks
howItManifests.symptom: `generateText` hangs during multi-tool orchestration on Vercel Edge
howItManifests.impact: Users experience blank streaming UIs after 30s timeout

detection.name: Stream Hallucination Detector
detection.description: Intercepts `streamText` chunks to flag semantic drift using embedding distance

detection.name: Tool Call Validation Monitor
detection.description: Inspects `streamObject` tool results for validation before UI render

detection.name: Timeout & Latency Tracker
detection.description: Measures `generateText` duration to detect edge timeouts

fixes.name: Chunk-Level Hallucination Filter
fixes.description: Buffers `streamText` chunks and filters hallucinations before UI render

fixes.name: Validated Tool Result Wrapper
fixes.description: Always validates tool outputs before streaming to UI

fixes.name: Edge-Optimized Agent Chunking
fixes.description: Breaks complex tasks into smaller `generateText` calls to avoid timeouts

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Observability Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex middleware automatically wraps Vercel AI SDK's `generateText` and `streamText` calls, injecting observability without code changes. It captures streaming chunks, tool call payloads, and latency metrics in real-time, using AI-powered detectors trained on Vercel AI SDK failure patterns. 

The SDK detects all four failure modes—hallucinations via semantic drift analysis, unvalidated tools through schema enforcement, structured output inconsistencies across providers, and edge timeouts with predictive scaling. Vex generates remediation code automatically and supports one-click rollout.

Integration takes 3 lines: install `@vex_dev/sdk`, wrap your AI calls with `vexMiddleware`, and view dashboards showing failure rates, traces, and auto-fixes for your Vercel deployments.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add 3 lines of Vex middleware to eliminate AI Agent observability gaps in your Vercel AI SDK app today.
